title: Charred Tender Jamaican Lardons Wrap
description: A flavorful and easy-to-make wrap featuring charred lardons, caramelized onions, and sweet Turkish delight, all wrapped in a delicate lotus leaf.

ingredients.name: Lardons
ingredients.notes: pork fat is traditional, but vegan alternatives work well too.

ingredients.name: Onion
ingredients.notes: sweet onions like Vidalia or Maui work best.

ingredients.name: Turkish delight
ingredients.notes: dried or fresh, diced into small pieces.

ingredients.name: Smoked paprika
ingredients.notes: optional, adds a nice smoky flavor.

ingredients.name: Scotch bonnet peppers
ingredients.notes: optional, can be omitted for less heat.

instructions: Heat a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the coconut oil and swirl to coat the bottom of the pan.
Add the lardons and cook, stirring occasionally, until they are browned and crispy, about 10 minutes.
Add the onions and cook, stirring occasionally, until they are caramelized and golden brown, about 15-20 minutes.
Add the cumin, coriander, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper to the pan, stirring to combine.
Add the Turkish delight to the pan and stir to combine.
If using scotch bonnet peppers, add them to the pan and stir to combine.
Meanwhile, prepare the lotus leaves by washing and drying them thoroughly.
To assemble the wraps, place a spoonful of the lardon mixture onto the center of a lotus leaf, followed by a spoonful of the caramelized onions and a sprinkle of chopped cilantro.
Drizzle with a squeeze of lime juice and serve immediately, with mashed avocado on the side, if desired.
